summary: Implement Rust→Python ASGI dispatch bridge with Depends() DI, shared event loop, include_router/mount/on_event support, and built-in HTTP fallback server. Extends cclab Python packages (log, pg, schema) for Conductor backend compatibility.
diff:
10 files changed, 549 insertions(+), 75 deletions(-)

## Key Changes

### python/cclab/api/app.py (+437/-41)
- Added `_get_shared_loop()` — shared event loop for Rust→Python handler bridge so DB connection pools work correctly across handlers
- Added `_wrap_handler_for_rust()` — wraps Python handlers for Rust server dispatch: extracts path_params, query_params, body from request dict; resolves Depends() DI (async generators, coroutines, sync generators, plain callables); maps remaining params from request; handles cleanup of async generators
- Modified `_register_route()` to wrap handlers with `_wrap_handler_for_rust()` before Rust registration
- Added `include_router()` — includes routes from FastAPI APIRouter, registers each route in both Python route table and Rust app with wrapped handlers
- Added `mount()` — stores mounted sub-applications for later ASGI integration
- Added `on_event()` — decorator for startup/shutdown event handlers
- Modified `add_middleware()` to support both cclab style (instance) and FastAPI style (class + kwargs)
- Modified `run()` — runs startup hooks on shared event loop before server start; improved fallback chain (uvicorn → built-in HTTP server)
- Added `_run_builtin()` — built-in asyncio HTTP server using HTTPServer/BaseHTTPRequestHandler (no uvicorn needed)
- Modified `__call__` ASGI dispatch — uses Rust matcher for path parameter support with regex fallback for router-added routes; adds path_params to kwargs resolution

### crates/cclab-pyo3/src/lib.rs (+22/-9)
- Replaced manual submodule aliases with `alias_pymodule!` and `alias_register!` macros
- Added aliases for http, log, api, titan, agent, crypto, runtime, schema, nebula, meteor — enables `from cclab.cclab import X` pattern for all packages

### python/cclab/log/__init__.py (+24/-2)
- Added `get_logger(name)` — returns Python stdlib logger (used by Conductor)
- Added `configure_logging(level)` — configures basic logging format

### python/cclab/pg/__init__.py (+59)
- Added ORM type re-exports from SQLAlchemy (DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship, String, Text, Integer, JSON, DateTime, ForeignKey, UniqueConstraint, Index, UUID)
- Added `Connection` compat wrapper around SQLAlchemy AsyncSession
- Added `Pool` compat wrapper around SQLAlchemy async engine + session factory
- Added `create_pool(url)` — creates async connection pool wrapping SQLAlchemy
- Added `Migration` class for SQL migration up/down callables

### python/cclab/schema/__init__.py (+16/-2)
- Prefer pydantic-settings BaseSettings when available (full env parsing + Field support)
- Re-export pydantic types: BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict, field_validator, model_validator, validator, TypeAdapter, ValidationError

### python/cclab/api/__init__.py (+13)
- Override cclab types with FastAPI equivalents when available (Header, Query, Body, Depends, BackgroundTasks, HTTPException)

### projects/conductor/be/main.py (+10/-8)
- Replaced `cclab.runtime.serve()` with `app.run()` — no uvicorn import needed
- Added `@app.on_event('startup')` for DB connection

### projects/conductor/be/src/database/database.py (+3/-4)
- Added lazy pool initialization in `get_session()` — auto-connects if pool not initialized

### projects/conductor/be/src/api/platform/deps.py (+2)
- Added lazy DB connect check in `get_db()` dependency

### .claude/skills/dev-server → conductor-dev-server (+1/-1)
- Renamed skill from dev-server to conductor-dev-server
test_results: Tests not yet run — implementation covers 10 commits (17785cee..5b43026b) across Python packages and Conductor backend. Key functionality verified manually: 39 Conductor routes registered, Rust server dispatch with DI resolution, shared event loop for DB connections.
notes: Implementation evolved over 10 commits from initial PyO3 alias fixes through full ASGI dispatch with DI resolution. The shared event loop pattern (_get_shared_loop) was the final fix ensuring DB connection pools work correctly when Rust server calls Python handlers synchronously via run_until_complete.
